Our Work

Projects that delivered
measurable outcomes

A selection of infrastructure and cloud projects delivered through Nuvextro's partner network. Client details anonymized where requested.

Virtualization · Enterprise

VMware vSphere Modernization for Regional Financial Institution

A regional bank running aging ESXi 6.0 infrastructure needed to modernize their virtualization platform to meet new compliance requirements while maintaining zero-downtime operations across 200+ VMs.

12 wks
Delivery time
0
Unplanned downtime events
40%
Infrastructure footprint reduction
Challenge/Approach/Outcome

The client was running an end-of-life VMware vSphere 6.0 environment across three physical sites. The aging infrastructure posed compliance risks, performance bottlenecks, and a complete absence of DR capability. Any migration had to maintain 99.99% uptime for core banking applications.

Approach

Nuvextro designed a phased migration strategy with a new vSphere 8.0 cluster built in parallel. Workloads were live-migrated using vMotion in carefully planned maintenance windows. NSX-T was introduced to modernize the network layer, and a Veeam-based replication strategy was implemented for DR.

Outcome

Full infrastructure modernization completed across 12 weeks with zero unplanned downtime. The client achieved full compliance with updated banking IT regulations, a 40% reduction in infrastructure footprint through improved VM density, and a functional DR capability with a 4-hour RTO.

Cloud Migration · Manufacturing

AWS Cloud Migration for Mid-Market Manufacturing Company

A 400-person manufacturing business needed to migrate 18 on-premises workloads to AWS to enable remote operations and reduce data center costs ahead of a facility consolidation.

18
Workloads migrated
10 wks
End-to-end delivery
$8.2k
Monthly cost reduction
Challenge/Approach/Outcome

The client had a mix of legacy Windows Server applications, SQL Server databases, and a custom ERP system — many of which had undocumented dependencies. The migration had to be completed within 3 months to align with the physical data center decommission schedule.

Approach

Nuvextro conducted a full application dependency mapping exercise using AWS Application Discovery Service. Workloads were grouped into migration waves by dependency and business criticality. A landing zone was built using AWS Control Tower with cost guardrails and security baselines. Migration was executed using AWS MGN and DMS for databases.

Outcome

All 18 workloads migrated successfully within the 10-week target. Monthly infrastructure costs reduced by $8,200 vs the on-premises equivalent. Post-migration, Nuvextro implemented a cost optimization review that identified a further $1,400/month in savings through rightsizing and reserved instance planning.

Disaster Recovery · Healthcare

Disaster Recovery Solution for Healthcare Services Provider

A healthcare organization needed a documented, tested disaster recovery capability for their patient data and clinical systems — a compliance requirement they had not previously met.

4hr
Recovery time objective
1hr
Recovery point objective
$620/mo
Ongoing operational cost
Challenge/Approach/Outcome

The client operated critical patient management and imaging systems with no formal DR plan. An upcoming audit required evidence of a documented recovery capability with defined RTO/RPO targets. Budget constraints ruled out a traditional secondary data center.

Approach

Nuvextro designed a cloud-based DR solution using Azure Site Recovery for primary clinical systems, with Veeam Cloud Connect for secondary backup targets. Recovery objectives were defined collaboratively with clinical IT leadership: 4-hour RTO and 1-hour RPO for critical systems. Full runbooks were developed, and a tabletop DR exercise was conducted with the client team.

Outcome

The client passed their compliance audit with the required DR documentation and evidence of a successful test recovery. Ongoing monthly DR testing is managed by Nuvextro as part of a retainer engagement. Total solution cost was under $3,000 to implement, with monthly operational costs of $620.
